deviations.summary: Open Bitcoin-only onboarding, service, dashboard, migration, metrics, logging, storage, and sync settings live in open-bitcoin.jsonc instead of bitcoin.conf.

migration_notice.text: Open Bitcoin-only settings stay in open-bitcoin.jsonc; the source bitcoin.conf remains read-only review input.

deviations.summary: Phase 21 migration is dry-run only. It does not disable source services, rewrite source datadirs, or perform automatic switch-over.

migration_notice.text: The current migration planner is review-only. Service cutover and source-data mutation remain manual follow-up work.

deviations.summary: Open Bitcoin exports managed-wallet backups as repo-owned JSON and does not copy, restore, or rewrite Core or Knots wallet.dat files.

migration_notice.text: Managed-wallet backup export is Open Bitcoin-owned JSON, not a Core-compatible wallet.dat copy or rewrite flow.

quirks: MoneyRange depends on the exact MAX_MONEY ceiling even though MAX_MONEY is a sanity bound rather than a supply counter.
base_blob and uint256 hex uses reverse-byte display order relative to typical byte-array tools.
CompactSize rejects non-canonical encodings and enforces MAX_SIZE while vector allocation grows in 5 MiB batches.
Transaction serialization keeps explicit witness and non-witness modes and rejects superfluous witness encodings.
CBlockLocator serializes an unused DUMMY_VERSION field for historical compatibility.
P2P relay keeps txid and wtxid distinctions visible through GenTxid, CInv, and WTXIDRELAY.
suspected_unknowns: Which in-scope interfaces still require deprecated hex-acceptance behavior instead of strict FromHex or FromUserHex parsing.
Which additional serializer parameter contexts beyond transaction witness mode need explicit Rust types once disk and networking adapters land.
Which protocol and address-codec edge cases need dedicated repo-owned fixtures beyond the imported unit and fuzz coverage.

quirks: Unspendable outputs stay out of the spendable UTXO view even when they appear in connected blocks.
Undo replay happens in reverse transaction and reverse input order during disconnect.
Best-chain choice is work-first, with a deterministic hash tie-break for repo-owned fixtures.
suspected_unknowns: How much of Knots' cache-flush and multi-chainstate behavior should remain pure-core versus adapter-owned in later phases.

quirks: Admission derives prevouts from chainstate first and then from already-accepted mempool parents, keeping the policy core free of hidden node globals.
Replacement requires a higher absolute fee, a higher feerate than each direct conflict, and an incremental relay-fee bump before the conflict set is removed.
Open Bitcoin recomputes parent-child and ancestor-descendant metrics deterministically after each mutation instead of mirroring Knots' cache-heavy bookkeeping in this first slice.
suspected_unknowns: How closely rolling minimum-fee decay and long-lived mempool pressure should mirror Knots once the node keeps a persistent runtime mempool.
How much package-relay behavior should remain pure-core versus move into later networking orchestration.

quirks: Compact target decoding rejects negative, zero, and overflowing targets before any hash comparison.
Merkle validation distinguishes plain root mismatch from duplicate-transaction malleation.
Transaction and block validation expose stable reject reasons instead of silent booleans.
Handshake keeps `wtxidrelay` and `sendheaders` visible as first-class capability messages instead of burying them in peer metadata.
Unknown block announcements trigger locator-driven header sync before block download, matching the modern header-first flow.
Transaction relay keeps txid and wtxid identity visible and switches requested inventory type when `wtxidrelay` is negotiated.
suspected_unknowns: How closely later phases should mirror Knots peer-eviction, timeout, and resource-governance policy once real transport adapters land.
Which discovery and address-relay behaviors should stay pure-core versus move into later shell-owned transport orchestration.

quirks: The first wallet slice keeps change explicit through internal descriptors instead of implicit keypool behavior.
Wallet balances and UTXOs rebuild from chainstate snapshots rather than coupling the wallet core to persistence.
Legacy, segwit-v0, and taproot signing reuse the canonical consensus sighash helpers instead of a second signing engine.
Phase 20 keeps the operator preview or confirm flow Open Bitcoin-owned while the final send mutation still goes through the wallet-scoped sendtoaddress path.
suspected_unknowns: How closely future HD, multisig, and PSBT flows should mirror Knots once the interface phase exposes descriptor imports.
Which parts of Knots' coin-selection and wallet metadata surface should stay pure-core versus move into later shell-owned orchestration.

documents.title: RPC, CLI, And Config Operator Surface

quirks: The supported operator slice is intentionally single-wallet and local-auth only; deferred surfaces are listed explicitly instead of implied by omission.
Open Bitcoin adds deterministic buildtransaction and buildandsigntransaction extension methods for the current wallet adapter slice.
The CLI keeps -getinfo as a four-call helper batch while normal method execution stays RPC-shaped.
Wallet-scoped RPC routing now covers -rpcwallet or /wallet/<name> for the shipped wallet subset while open-bitcoin keeps preview or backup workflows outside the baseline-compatible parser.
suspected_unknowns: How closely later phases should mirror Knots sendtoaddress, getpeerinfo, and multiwallet semantics once the adapter-owned wallet and peer surfaces grow.

quirks: The same Rust functional case list can run against Open Bitcoin or an external Knots-compatible JSON-RPC endpoint.
Open Bitcoin parity coverage runs by default; Knots-backed execution is opt-in through explicit environment variables.
Property-style tests are deterministic generated tests under normal cargo test rather than a separate fuzzing runtime in this phase.
suspected_unknowns: How much of the upstream Python functional suite should be translated versus wrapped once a built Knots daemon path is available.
Which parser or protocol surfaces deserve a dedicated fuzzing runtime beyond the deterministic generated tests.

quirks: Migration planning is an Open Bitcoin-owned operator workflow rather than a baseline-compatible bitcoin-cli parser surface.
Detected source datadirs, services, configs, cookies, and wallet candidates stay read-only throughout the Phase 21 planner.
Relevant intentional differences are surfaced alongside the plan rather than hidden in docs-only audit artifacts.
suspected_unknowns: How closely a future apply-mode migration should mirror Knots once source-data mutation becomes in scope.
Which live-sync or runtime cutover checks should be mandatory before an operator can safely disable the source service.

quirks: Repo-native verification stays offline by default and validates the generated smoke benchmark report instead of trusting artifact existence alone.
Smoke benchmarks use the debug profile while full local benchmarks use a release build, and both remain threshold-free evidence rather than release gates.
The operator-facing release narrative keeps migration dry-run-only boundaries and other non-claims visible instead of implying automatic cutover parity.
suspected_unknowns: Which packaged or signed release workflow should anchor a later install surface beyond the current source-built path.
Whether any public-network sync verification should become optional release evidence in a later milestone without expanding the default local gate.

surfaces.title: Reference Baseline
surfaces.rationale: The pinned Bitcoin Knots baseline and deviation ledger are established for the initial milestone.

surfaces.title: Architecture And Workspace Guardrails
surfaces.rationale: First-party workspace, architecture-policy checks, and repo-native verification are in place.

surfaces.title: Core Domain And Serialization
surfaces.rationale: The typed domain and codec foundations are cataloged and verified for the initial milestone surface.
suspected_unknowns: Which user-facing interfaces still require deprecated hex acceptance instead of stricter parsing.
Which serializer parameter contexts beyond transaction witness mode need explicit Rust types.
Which protocol and address-codec edge cases need additional repo-owned fixtures.

surfaces.title: Consensus Validation
surfaces.rationale: Consensus execution and later parity gap closures have verification evidence for the in-scope milestone surface.

surfaces.title: Chainstate And UTXO Engine
surfaces.rationale: The pure chainstate, UTXO, connect, disconnect, and reorg slice is implemented and cataloged.
known_gaps: Disk-backed coins databases, cache-flush policy, assumeutxo flows, mempool repair, and full chainstate-manager behavior remain outside the current slice.
suspected_unknowns: How much of Knots cache-flush and multi-chainstate behavior should stay pure-core versus adapter-owned later.

surfaces.title: Mempool Policy
surfaces.rationale: Admission, replacement, accounting, eviction, and policy deviations are verified and cataloged for the current surface.
known_gaps: Package relay, rolling minimum-fee decay, reorg-driven repair, and operator-facing mempool interfaces remain follow-up surfaces.
suspected_unknowns: How closely long-lived mempool pressure and package-relay behavior should mirror Knots once runtime state grows.

surfaces.title: P2P Networking And Sync
surfaces.rationale: Peer lifecycle, wire handling, header and block sync, and relay behavior are covered for the current in-memory surface.
known_gaps: Address relay, addrv2, discovery, compact blocks, encrypted transport, peer eviction, bans, and long-running socket orchestration remain deferred surfaces.
suspected_unknowns: How closely peer-eviction, timeout, resource-governance, discovery, and address-relay behavior should mirror Knots later.

surfaces.title: Wallet Core And Adapters
surfaces.rationale: The shipped wallet slice now includes ranged single-key descriptors, durable named-wallet selection, freshness or rescan visibility, sendtoaddress-style sends, operator preview or confirm flows, and Open Bitcoin-owned backup export.
known_gaps: Miniscript, multisig, PSBT, encryption, external signer, restore or import compatibility with external wallet formats, and full multiwallet lifecycle parity remain outside the current slice.
suspected_unknowns: Which future HD, multisig, PSBT, coin-selection, and metadata behaviors should stay pure-core versus shell-owned.

surfaces.title: RPC, CLI, And Config
surfaces.rationale: The supported local JSON-RPC, bitcoin-cli, config, auth, wallet-scoped routing, and headless operator workflow slice is verified.
known_gaps: Richer wallet-send ergonomics beyond the shipped sendtoaddress path, getpeerinfo, -netinfo, full multiwallet lifecycle parity, rpcauth, rpcwhitelist, rpcwait, daemon supervision, and broader process-control helpers remain deferred.
suspected_unknowns: How closely richer wallet-send ergonomics, getpeerinfo, and full multiwallet semantics should mirror Knots when the adapter-owned surfaces grow.

surfaces.title: Drop-In Audit And Migration
surfaces.rationale: Phase 21 makes the replacement story auditable through a dry-run migration planner, an explicit drop-in audit matrix, and surfaced intentional differences.
known_gaps: Automatic service cutover, source-datadir mutation, external-wallet import or restore, and any full drop-in replacement claim remain outside the current slice.
suspected_unknowns: How closely a future apply-mode migration should mirror Knots once destructive paths become in scope.
Which runtime validation checks should gate a later manual or automated cutover.

surfaces.title: Verification Harnesses And Fuzzing
surfaces.rationale: The shared parity harness, isolation helpers, deterministic property tests, and CI report collection are verified.
known_gaps: The harness does not yet build or spawn the vendored Knots daemon, translate the full upstream Python suite, or use a dedicated cargo-fuzz/libFuzzer runtime.
suspected_unknowns: How much of the upstream Python functional suite should be translated versus wrapped.
Which parser or protocol surfaces deserve a dedicated fuzzing runtime beyond deterministic generated tests.

surfaces.title: Real-Sync Benchmarks
surfaces.rationale: Phase 22 extends the benchmark registry with deterministic runtime-backed sync, storage, status, dashboard, and wallet-rescan evidence while keeping the smoke path bounded and threshold-free.
known_gaps: Benchmark reports remain audit evidence rather than release timing gates.
Public-network sync benchmarks stay outside the default local verification contract.
suspected_unknowns: Which future runtime scenarios should become benchmark cases if packaging, install, or live-network validation expands.

surfaces.title: Operator Runtime Release Hardening
surfaces.rationale: The current v1.1 operator runtime now has repo-owned documentation, verification, and parity-ledger evidence that keeps shipped behavior separate from deferred or out-of-scope work.
known_gaps: Packaged install flows, Windows service support, unattended public-mainnet full sync through open-bitcoind, migration apply mode, and hosted dashboards remain outside the v1.1 release-hardened slice; Phase 35 adds opt-in daemon sync activation and durable preflight only.
suspected_unknowns: Which future packaging or signed-release workflow should become the canonical install surface once source-built operation is no longer the only path.
Whether optional public-network release evidence should eventually supplement the current offline verification contract.
Which post-v1.2 milestones should extend daemon sync into broader P2P policy, wallet, production-service, or package-install claims.
